Draft Strategy Board

Strategies authored against ADP snapshot: Underdog ADP via ffdataroma_draft_guide_export/ffdataroma/csv/underdog-adp.csv (top-216 coverage 96.8% per slot_paths._meta); adj_rank from flag_ranks.json

Stack pieces average +4.7 picks ahead of ADP by slot structure — capital chips mark each pick; wait where your room allows.

Evidence base: WINNING_STRUCTURE.md

12 draft slots 36 strategies 18 rounds 2026-07-02T20:45:44Z authored rendered 2026-07-02T22:25:58Z
Slot 1Picks: 1, 24, 25…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: the full Tampa stack is live from slot 1 — Baker Mayfield R10 (pick 120) + Emeka Egbuka R2 (pick 24) + Jalen McMillan R12; TB is ELITE with the pool's #2 stack score (0.812) and all-value pieces. NYG (Jaxson Dart R8 + Malik Nabers R2-3) is the deepest-value alternative.
THESIS
Pick 1 is the only slot that locks Jahmyr Gibbs (adj_rank 1, ELITE Detroit) as a bell-cow. BBM VI favored early RB (R1 RB ~24% advance; two consecutive/early RBs ~30%). We bank Gibbs, take a value 2nd RB by R5 to run a true early-RB build, and pair a value Dallas stack (Dak+Pickens+Ferguson) that carries a DAL@NYG Week-17 game (env 73.1), plus a cheap KC secondary.
Primary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6-7, ADP~80, value adj77)
Pieces: George Pickens (R2, ADP~24) + Jake Ferguson (R11, value)
Rounds: Pickens R2, Dak R6-7, Ferguson R11
Windows: George Pickens R2–R2 · prefer R2 (54% · +0.3, unavoidable-premium); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (75% · +6.6, flexible); Jake Ferguson R10–R11 · prefer R11 (68% · +7.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (DAL@NYG env 73.1)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8-9 if at/under market)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R3, value) + Xavier Worthy
Rounds: Rice R3, Mahomes R8-9
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R3 · prefer R3 (52% · +0.1, unavoidable-premium); Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (39% · -3.1, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston / McConkey (KC@LAC env 76.6)

W17 CORRELATIONDAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens+Ferguson, bring back Nabers, env 73.1) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) give two Week-17 games; projects 5-7 correlated pieces.
The slot's early-RB build: spends pick 1 on the RB anchor and adds a 2nd RB by R5; DAL+KC stacks. Distinct from 1B (WR-forward CIN, no R1 RB) and 1C (elite-TE McBride leverage, DET).
THESIS
Take the elite-WR lane: Ja'Marr Chase (ELITE CIN, W17 vs BAL) at 1.01, then a BAL@CIN Week-17 concentrator. Push WR quantity early (Chase + wheel WRs), satisfy the hard RB1<=R7 floor with deep R6-7 RB value. Stacks Burrow+Chase+Higgins with a Lamar+Flowers bring-back — a self-contained final-week game-stack (env 75.9).
Primary Stack
CIN
QB: Joe Burrow (R5, ADP~59)
Pieces: Ja'Marr Chase (R1) + Tee Higgins (R3, value)
Rounds: Chase R1, Higgins R3, Burrow R5
Windows: Tee Higgins R2–R3 · prefer R3 (99% · +10.4, conscious-stack-premium); Joe Burrow R5–R5 · prefer R5 (92% · +10.1, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: BAL Mark Andrews (R11, value) — completes BAL@CIN (env 75.9)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8-9, at/under market)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8-9
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R2 · prefer R2 (65% · +1.1, unavoidable-premium); Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (39% · -3.1, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston (KC@LAC env 76.6)

Joe Burrow taken R5 (pick 49) — CIN game-stack head (Chase+Higgins). R5 is his only window at slot 1; by the R6 wheel he is gone. qb_late=false is a conscious, game-stack-driven exception, not a passed check.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN (Burrow+Chase+Higgins, bring back Andrews, env 75.9) is a 4-player self-contained Week-17 game-stack; KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) adds a second game. Projects 5-7 game-stacked players.
WR-forward, NO first-round RB, CIN primary + BAL bring-back — archetype differs from 1A (Hero-RB) and 1C (early elite-TE McBride); CIN core is distinct from 1A's DAL emphasis.
THESIS
A deliberate contrarian: pair the pick-1 Gibbs anchor with Trey McBride (adj 22, value) on the pick-24 wheel — the only slot where the #1 RB and a top-2 TE are both reachable in two picks. WINNING_STRUCTURE flags elite TE as normally -EV; this is a conscious leverage cost taken ONLY because McBride returns at value (adj22<ADP24). The PRIMARY correlation is Detroit itself — Gibbs's own offense — via Goff+Jameson, a CHI@DET Week-17 game (env 81.0); Dallas is the secondary. We punt QB late and build WR depth.
Primary Stack
DET
QB: Jared Goff (R8, ADP~104, value adj103)
Pieces: Jameson Williams (R4, value) — correlates the Gibbs home game
Rounds: Jamo R4, Goff R8
Windows: Jameson Williams R4–R5 · prefer R5 (72% · +3.6, flexible); Jared Goff R8–R9 · prefer R9 (71% · +7.0, flexible)
W17 bring-back: CHI Loveland / Rome Odunze (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6-7, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R3) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R3, Dak R6-7, Ferguson R11
Windows: George Pickens R2–R3 · prefer R3 (41% · -0.7, flexible); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (75% · +6.6, flexible)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (DAL@NYG env 73.1)

RB1 R1QB1 R7TE1 R2
W17 CORRELATIONDAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) and CHI@DET (Gibbs+Jamo+Goff, bring back Loveland, env 81.0) supply game-stacks; McBride is an uncorrelated leverage TE. Projects ~4-6 game-stacked players — the leverage trade-off.
Only build using an early elite TE (McBride) as leverage; stacks DAL+DET. Clearly distinct from 1A (KC secondary, no early TE) and 1B (CIN, no R1 RB).
Slot 2Picks: 2, 23, 26…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: full TB stack reachable — Mayfield R10 + Egbuka R3 + Chris Godwin R7 (ELITE, stack score 0.812, value pieces). NYG Dart R8 + Nabers R3 is the value alternative; 2B's PHI/NYG core already leans off-chalk.
THESIS
Slot 2 lands Bijan Robinson (adj 2) as a bell-cow RB anchor, satisfying the early-RB lean immediately. From the 23/26 wheel we build a WR tower and a value KC (Mahomes+Rice) stack, with a Dallas secondary. RB1 locked in R1 so every later pick chases ceiling and correlation.
Primary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8-9, ADP~93)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy (R9-10)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8-9, Worthy R10
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R3 · prefer R3 (39% · -0.9, flexible); Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (43% · -2.1, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston / McConkey (KC@LAC env 76.6)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6-7, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R2/R3) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R3, Dak R6-7
Windows: George Pickens R2–R2 · prefer R2 (67% · +1.3, unavoidable-premium); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (72% · +5.6, flexible)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)

R1

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice+Worthy, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces.
Hero-RB on Bijan with KC+DAL stacks. Distinct from 2B (WR-forward JSN/SEA+CIN) and 2C (McCaffrey robust-RB).
THESIS
Open elite-WR (Jaxon Smith-Njigba, R1) to avoid the R1 RB, then build a value Philadelphia stack (Hurts R6 + DeVonta R2 + Goedert R10) that carries a PHI@SF Week-17 game (env 66.8), with an NYG (Dart+Nabers) secondary that shares Dallas's W17 opponent (DAL@NYG env 73.1). RB1 floor met by R6-7 value.
Primary Stack
PHI
QB: Jalen Hurts (R6-7, ADP~76, value adj73)
Pieces: DeVonta Smith (R2, value) + Dallas Goedert (R10, value)
Rounds: DeVonta R2, Hurts R6-7, Goedert R10
Windows: DeVonta Smith R2–R3 · prefer R2 (93% · +5.1, unavoidable-premium); Jalen Hurts R6–R7 · prefer R7 (58% · +1.9, flexible); Dallas Goedert R10–R11 · prefer R11 (56% · +2.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: SF George Kittle / Ricky Pearsall (PHI@SF env 66.8)
Secondary Stack
NYG
QB: Jaxson Dart (R8-9, ADP~102, value)
Pieces: Malik Nabers (R3, value)
Rounds: Nabers R3, Dart R8-9
Windows: Malik Nabers R3–R3 · prefer R3 (99% · +11.6, conscious-stack-premium); Jaxson Dart R8–R9 · prefer R9 (63% · +3.9, flexible)
W17 bring-back: DAL George Pickens / CeeDee Lamb (DAL@NYG env 73.1)

W17 CORRELATIONPHI@SF (Hurts+DeVonta+Goedert, bring back Kittle, env 66.8) and DAL@NYG (Dart+Nabers, bring back Pickens/Lamb, env 73.1) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
WR-forward, no R1 RB, PHI+NYG stacks — different archetype AND offenses from 2A (KC/DAL) and 2C (SF/KC robust).
THESIS
Christian McCaffrey (adj 6, HIGH SF) is reachable at pick 2; build a robust-RB spine (2 RB by R3) to bank the early-RB edge, then a Brock Purdy (R8, value) stack that correlates with the CMC game, plus a KC secondary. Robust-RB is regime-dependent (§2.5) so WR quantity stays high from R4.
Primary Stack
SF
QB: Brock Purdy (R8, ADP~97, value)
Pieces: George Kittle (R9-10, value) + Ricky Pearsall / Mike Evans (R4-5)
Rounds: Evans R4-5, Purdy R8, Kittle R9-10
Windows: Mike Evans R4–R4 · prefer R4 (72% · +3.5, unavoidable-premium); Brock Purdy R8–R8 · prefer R8 (58% · +2.3, unavoidable-premium); George Kittle R8–R9 · prefer R9 (70% · +6.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: PHI DeVonta Smith (PHI@SF env 66.8)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8-9)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R9
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R2 · prefer R2 (76% · +2.1, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Johnston (env 76.6)

RB1 R1QB1 R8TE1 R9
W17 CORRELATIONPHI@SF (Purdy+Evans+Kittle, bring back DeVonta Smith, env 66.8) plus KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice) secondary; ~5-7 game-stacked pieces. CMC anchors the SF game correlation.
Robust-RB (CMC + Kyren) with SF/KC stacks — opposite archetype to 2B (WR-forward) and different offenses from 2A (KC/DAL primary emphasis).
Slot 3Picks: 3, 22, 27…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: 3A already runs the TB side (Mayfield R10 + Egbuka R3 + Godwin R7) — treat it as the leverage build. CHI (newly ELITE) is the alternative: Caleb Williams R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R4.
THESIS
Puka Nacua (value adj3, HIGH LAR) at pick 3 opens one of the best Week-17 correlations in the pool: LAR plays TB in Week 17 — TB is ELITE, LAR is HIGH rank 7 (game env 77.8). Stafford's ADP falls in slot 3's dead zone (unreachable), so we QB the game from Tampa — Baker Mayfield (R10-11) tied to Egbuka+Godwin — and roster Nacua+Adams from LAR as the same-game correlation. A 5-piece single-game final-week stack. RB1 floor met by R6-7 value.
Primary Stack
TB
QB: Baker Mayfield (R10-11, ADP~121; take at/under market)
Pieces: Emeka Egbuka (R3, value) + Chris Godwin Jr. (R7, value)
Rounds: Egbuka R3, Godwin R7, Mayfield R10-11
Windows: Emeka Egbuka R3–R3 · prefer R3 (92% · +5.6, unavoidable-premium); Chris Godwin Jr. R7–R7 · prefer R7 (83% · +9.8, unavoidable-premium); Baker Mayfield R10–R10 · prefer R10 (59% · +3.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAR Puka Nacua (R1) + Davante Adams (R4) — same game (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
LAR
QB: (Stafford unreachable at slot 3) Nacua+Adams as the LAR side of the mirror
Pieces: Puka Nacua (R1) + Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Nacua R1, Adams R4
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (58% · +1.2, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Egbuka/Godwin (same game — double stack)

W17 CORRELATIONSignature build: LAR@TB single-game double-stack (Mayfield+Egbuka+Godwin from TB, Nacua+Adams from LAR) — 5 players in one 77.8-env final game. Highest single-game Week-17 concentration in the pool. Second late QB (R12) satisfies the 2-QB floor.
Unique LAR@TB mirror, WR-forward, QB'd from Tampa. Distinct from 3B (Robust-RB CMC + KC/SF) and 3C (Chase CIN/BAL).
THESIS
Christian McCaffrey (adj 6) reachable at pick 3; build a robust-RB spine (2 RB by R3, 3 by R6) to bank the early-RB advance edge, then pivot to a KC Mahomes stack for ceiling with an SF correlation to the CMC game. Robust-RB is regime-dependent (§2.5) so WR quantity stays high from R4.
Primary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8-9)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8-9
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R2 · prefer R2 (85% · +3.1, unavoidable-premium); Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (46% · -1.1, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston (KC@LAC env 76.6)
Secondary Stack
SF
QB: Brock Purdy (R8-9, value) — correlates the CMC game
Pieces: George Kittle (R9-10) / Ricky Pearsall
Rounds: Purdy R8, Kittle R9-10
Windows: Brock Purdy R8–R9 · prefer R9 (44% · -1.7, flexible)
W17 bring-back: PHI DeVonta Smith (PHI@SF env 66.8)

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) primary; PHI@SF (Purdy+Kittle, bring back DeVonta) correlates with the CMC game (env 66.8). ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Robust-RB (CMC + Kyren) with KC/SF stacks — opposite archetype to 3A (WR-forward LAR/TB) and different offenses from 3C (CIN).
THESIS
Ja'Marr Chase (adj 4, ELITE CIN) at pick 3 keys a BAL@CIN Week-17 concentrator (env 75.9). Stack Burrow+Chase+Higgins, bring back Flowers+Andrews from Baltimore. WR-forward core, RB1 by R7. A cleaner single-game alternative to 3A's mirror.
Primary Stack
CIN
QB: Joe Burrow (R5, ADP~59)
Pieces: Ja'Marr Chase (R1) + Tee Higgins (R3, value)
Rounds: Chase R1, Higgins R3, Burrow R5
Windows: Tee Higgins R3–R3 · prefer R3 (98% · +8.4, unavoidable-premium); Joe Burrow R5–R5 · prefer R5 (87% · +8.1, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: BAL Mark Andrews (R11, value) — completes BAL@CIN (env 75.9)
Secondary Stack
BAL
QB: Lamar Jackson (R4-5, value adj52) — the OTHER QB in the BAL@CIN final game
Pieces: Mark Andrews (R11, value)
Rounds: Lamar R4, Andrews R11
Windows: Lamar Jackson R4–R5 · prefer R4 (87% · +7.3, unavoidable-premium); Mark Andrews R11–R11 · prefer R11 (70% · +8.5, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CIN Chase/Higgins (same game — two-QB game-stack)

Lamar Jackson taken R4 at value (adj<=ADP) — the BAL side of the deliberate two-QB BAL@CIN Week-17 mirror (env 75.9). R4-5 is his only window at slot 3. Conscious exception to QB-late.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN two-QB game-stack (Burrow+Chase+Higgins vs Lamar+Andrews) — 5 correlated players incl. BOTH QBs in a 75.9-env final game; the evidence rewards 2-3 game-stacked QBs. Conscious early 2nd QB (Lamar R4) is the cost of the mirror.
CIN+BAL two-QB mirror, WR-forward. Distinct offenses from 3A (LAR/TB) and archetype from 3B (robust-RB).
Slot 4Picks: 4, 21, 28…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: full TB stack reachable (Mayfield R10 + Egbuka R2 + Godwin R7, ELITE, score 0.812); or CHI newly ELITE (Caleb R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R4). Either replaces the KC/DAL chalk without losing a premium W17 game.
THESIS
Slot 4 can land Amon-Ra St. Brown (adj7, ELITE Detroit) at R1 — the anchor for the best late-QB value stack in the pool (Goff R9, adj103<ADP104, score 0.894). Build ARSB + Jameson Williams + Goff and bring back Chicago (Loveland/Odunze) for a CHI@DET Week-17 game (env 81.0, the highest). RB1 floor met by R6-7 value.
Primary Stack
DET
QB: Jared Goff (R9, ADP~104, value adj103, qb_late)
Pieces: Amon-Ra St. Brown (R1) + Jameson Williams (R4, value)
Rounds: ARSB R1, Jamo R4, Goff R9
Windows: Jameson Williams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (89% · +7.6, unavoidable-premium); Jared Goff R9–R9 · prefer R9 (62% · +4.0, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CHI Colston Loveland / Rome Odunze (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R2, value) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R2, Dak R6
Windows: George Pickens R2–R2 · prefer R2 (87% · +3.3, unavoidable-premium); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (65% · +3.6, flexible); Jake Ferguson R11–R11 · prefer R11 (61% · +4.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (DAL@NYG env 73.1)

W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Goff+ARSB+Jamo, bring back Loveland/Odunze, env 81.0 — highest) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; projects 6-8 correlated pieces.
WR-forward with the DET home stack (only slots 4-9 can anchor ARSB in R1). Distinct from 4B (Hero-RB CMC + LAR) and 4C (Jefferson leverage + CIN).
THESIS
Christian McCaffrey (adj6) at pick 4 banks a bell-cow; we add a value 2nd RB by R5 for a true early-RB build, then attack the LAR@TB mirror (env 77.8) — Stafford (R7, value) + Adams from LAR, with Egbuka+Godwin+Mayfield from TB. The early-RB lean plus the pool's top Week-17 game env.
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7, ADP~89, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (65% · +2.2, unavoidable-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R7 · prefer R7 (89% · +13.3, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Emeka Egbuka (R3) / Chris Godwin (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: Baker Mayfield (R10, at/under market)
Pieces: Emeka Egbuka (R3, value) + Chris Godwin
Rounds: Egbuka R3, Mayfield R10
Windows: Emeka Egbuka R2–R3 · prefer R3 (88% · +4.6, unavoidable-premium); Baker Mayfield R10–R10 · prefer R10 (61% · +4.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game)

R1

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB single-game double-stack (Stafford+Adams from LAR, Mayfield+Egbuka+Godwin from TB) — 5 players in a 77.8-env final. CMC adds the PHI@SF game if a SF bring-back is grabbed late.
The slot's early-RB build (CMC + 2nd RB by R5) with the LAR/TB mirror — opposite archetype to 4A (WR-forward DET) and different offenses from 4C (WR leverage, CIN).
THESIS
Justin Jefferson (adj9) at pick 4 is a leverage WR — elite talent on a LOW-ceiling-tier Minnesota offense that the field fades; his spike-week ceiling is the upside (WINNING_STRUCTURE leverage principle). We stack the field-favored BAL@CIN game instead (Burrow+Higgins, bring back Flowers/Andrews) for our correlation. RB1 by R7.
Primary Stack
CIN
QB: Joe Burrow (R5, value window)
Pieces: Tee Higgins (R3, value) + Chase Brown (RB, R2 — bonus)
Rounds: Higgins R3, Burrow R5
Windows: Tee Higgins R3–R3 · prefer R3 (96% · +7.4, unavoidable-premium); Joe Burrow R5–R5 · prefer R5 (84% · +7.1, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: BAL Mark Andrews (R11, value) — BAL@CIN env 75.9
Secondary Stack
BAL
QB: Lamar Jackson (R4, value adj52) — other QB in the BAL@CIN game
Pieces: Mark Andrews (R11, value)
Rounds: Lamar R4, Andrews R11
Windows: Lamar Jackson R4–R5 · prefer R4 (90% · +8.3, unavoidable-premium); Mark Andrews R11–R11 · prefer R11 (68% · +7.5, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CIN Higgins (same game)

Lamar Jackson taken R4 at value — BAL side of the two-QB BAL@CIN Week-17 mirror (env 75.9). R4 is his window at slot 4. Conscious exception to QB-late.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN two-QB game-stack (Burrow+Higgins vs Lamar+Andrews) — 4-5 correlated pieces incl. both QBs in a 75.9-env final. Jefferson is an uncorrelated leverage WR. Deliberate early 2nd QB (Lamar R4).
WR-forward leverage (Jefferson) + CIN/BAL two-QB mirror. Distinct offenses from 4A (DET/DAL) and archetype from 4B (Hero-RB).
Slot 5Picks: 5, 20, 29…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: last slot where the full TB stack is live (Mayfield R10 + Egbuka R3 + Godwin R7). CHI (Caleb R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R4, newly ELITE) and NYG (Dart R9 + Nabers R3) are the value alternatives.
THESIS
Jonathan Taylor (adj8) at pick 5 is a workhorse RB anchor; we bank him then build a value KC stack (Mahomes R8 + Rice R2) that carries a KC@LAC Week-17 game (env 76.6), with a Dallas secondary. Hero-RB satisfies the early-RB lean and frees the rest of the board for WR ceiling.
Primary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8, ADP~93, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy (R9-10)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8, Worthy R10
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R2 · prefer R2 (96% · +5.1, unavoidable-premium); Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (53% · +0.9, unavoidable-premium); Xavier Worthy R9–R9 · prefer R9 (72% · +7.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston / McConkey (KC@LAC env 76.6)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R2/R3) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R3, Dak R6
Windows: Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (61% · +2.6, flexible)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice+Worthy, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces.
Hero-RB (JT) with KC+DAL stacks. Distinct from 5B (WR-forward ARSB DET) and 5C (Robust-RB CMC/Henry).
THESIS
Amon-Ra St. Brown (adj7) at pick 5 anchors the elite Goff value stack (R9). Build ARSB+Jameson+Goff for a CHI@DET Week-17 game (env 81.0) and add a value Philadelphia secondary (Hurts+DeVonta). WR-forward; RB1 floor honored by R6-7.
Primary Stack
DET
QB: Jared Goff (R9, value adj103, qb_late)
Pieces: Amon-Ra St. Brown (R1) + Jameson Williams (R4, value)
Rounds: ARSB R1, Jamo R4, Goff R9
Windows: Jameson Williams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (91% · +8.6, unavoidable-premium); Jared Goff R9–R9 · prefer R9 (59% · +3.0, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CHI Loveland / Odunze (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
PHI
QB: Jalen Hurts (R6, value)
Pieces: DeVonta Smith (R2, value) + Dallas Goedert (R10)
Rounds: DeVonta R2, Hurts R6
Windows: DeVonta Smith R2–R2 · prefer R2 (99% · +8.1, unavoidable-premium); Jalen Hurts R6–R6 · prefer R6 (81% · +7.9, unavoidable-premium); Dallas Goedert R10–R10 · prefer R10 (71% · +8.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: SF George Kittle (PHI@SF env 66.8)

RB1 R5QB1 R6TE1 R10
W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Goff+ARSB+Jamo, bring back Loveland, env 81.0) and PHI@SF (Hurts+DeVonta+Goedert, bring back Kittle, env 66.8) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces.
WR-forward DET+PHI stacks. Distinct from 5A (Hero-RB KC/DAL) and 5C (Robust-RB).
THESIS
A robust-RB build off the pick-5 turn: Christian McCaffrey (R1) then Derrick Henry (R2, adj21, HIGH BAL) — two workhorse anchors banking the strongest early-RB signal (two consecutive RBs ~30% advance in BBM VI). Then a Ravens stack (Lamar+Flowers) that correlates Henry's own offense, with a KC secondary. WR quantity from R3.
Primary Stack
BAL
QB: Lamar Jackson (R4, value adj52) — correlates our Henry anchor
Pieces: Zay Flowers (R3, value) + Mark Andrews (R11)
Rounds: Flowers R3, Lamar R4, Andrews R11
Windows: Zay Flowers R2–R3 · prefer R3 (79% · +3.1, unavoidable-premium); Lamar Jackson R4–R4 · prefer R4 (93% · +9.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CIN Tee Higgins (BAL@CIN env 75.9)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (53% · +0.9, unavoidable-premium); Xavier Worthy R9–R9 · prefer R9 (72% · +7.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Johnston (env 76.6)

RB1 R1QB1 R4TE1 R11
Lamar Jackson taken R4 at value — heads the BAL stack that correlates the Derrick Henry R2 anchor (same offense) into the BAL@CIN final game. R4 is his window at slot 5. Conscious exception to QB-late.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN (Lamar+Flowers+Henry+Andrews, bring back Higgins, env 75.9) is a heavy same-game stack correlating the Henry anchor; KC@LAC secondary. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces. Deliberate early 2nd... (Lamar R4 is 1st QB; Mahomes R8 2nd).
Robust-RB (CMC + Henry) with a Henry-correlated BAL stack — opposite archetype to 5A/5B and unique in stacking the RB anchor's own offense.
Slot 6Picks: 6, 19, 30…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: TB's QB is out of range from slot 6 — CHI is the de-chalk: newly ELITE, Caleb Williams R6 + Colston Loveland R3 + Rome Odunze R5, feeding the highest-env W17 game (CHI@DET 81.0). NYG Dart R9 + Nabers R3 is the value alternative.
THESIS
Slot 6 sits in the RB-rich R1 band; we take an every-down back (Ashton Jeanty or James Cook, adj12/11) and pair a second RB (Derrick Henry / Kyren on the wheel) for the early-RB advance edge (two consecutive RBs ~30% in BBM VI). Stack Dallas (Dak+Pickens+Ferguson) for a DAL@NYG Week-17 game (env 73.1) and add a KC secondary.
Primary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6, ADP~80, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R2, value) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R2, Dak R6, Ferguson R11
Windows: George Pickens R2–R2 · prefer R2 (96% · +5.3, unavoidable-premium); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (57% · +1.6, flexible)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (DAL@NYG env 73.1)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2/R3, value) + Xavier Worthy
Rounds: Rice R3, Mahomes R8
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (57% · +1.9, unavoidable-premium); Xavier Worthy R9–R9 · prefer R9 (70% · +6.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Johnston (env 76.6)

W17 CORRELATIONDAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens+Ferguson, bring back Nabers, env 73.1) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Early-RB (Jeanty + Kyren) with DAL+KC stacks. Distinct from 6B (WR-forward ARSB DET) and 6C (Hero-RB Saquon + LAR).
THESIS
Amon-Ra St. Brown (adj7) at pick 6 anchors the premier Goff value stack (R9, score 0.894). ARSB+Jameson+Goff feeds a CHI@DET Week-17 game (env 81.0, highest), with a Chicago bring-back (Loveland/Odunze) and a value Dallas secondary. WR-forward; RB1 by R7.
Primary Stack
DET
QB: Jared Goff (R9, value adj103, qb_late)
Pieces: Amon-Ra St. Brown (R1) + Jameson Williams (R4, value)
Rounds: ARSB R1, Jamo R4, Goff R9
Windows: Jameson Williams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (94% · +9.6, unavoidable-premium); Jared Goff R9–R9 · prefer R9 (56% · +2.0, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: CHI Colston Loveland (R3) / Rome Odunze (CHI@DET env 81.0)

DAL

Pieces: George Pickens (R2, value)
Rounds: Pickens R2, Dak R6
Windows: George Pickens R2–R2 · prefer R2 (96% · +5.3, unavoidable-premium); Dak Prescott R6–R7 · prefer R7 (57% · +1.6, flexible)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)

RB1 R5QB1 R7TE1 R3
W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Goff+ARSB+Jamo vs Loveland/Odunze, env 81.0) is a 5-piece single-game Week-17 stack; DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens) adds a second game. ~6-8 correlated pieces. Loveland at R3 is an early TE but it is the game-stack bring-back, taken at value.
WR-forward DET home stack with a CHI bring-back TE. Distinct from 6A (early-RB DAL) and 6C (Hero-RB Saquon LAR).
THESIS
Saquon Barkley (adj13, HIGH PHI) at pick 6 is a bell-cow anchor whose own Eagles offense also gives a PHI@SF game. We pair the LAR@TB mirror (Stafford R7 + Adams, Egbuka+Godwin bring-back, env 77.8) as the primary correlation. Hero-RB satisfies the early-RB lean.
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (77% · +4.2, unavoidable-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R7 · prefer R7 (85% · +11.3, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Emeka Egbuka (R3) / Chris Godwin (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: (Mayfield unreachable at slot 6) Egbuka+Godwin as LAR-game correlators
Pieces: Emeka Egbuka (R3, value) + Chris Godwin (R7)
Rounds: Egbuka R3, Godwin R7
Windows: Emeka Egbuka R3–R3 · prefer R3 (74% · +2.6, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game)
18-ROUND PLAN
R1
RBSaquon BarkleyHIGHMODEL EDGE+7 early
ADP 13.4adj 13PHIW17 vs SF
PivotsRBChase BrownCIN16.9MODEL EDGERBDe'Von AchaneMIA19.1MODEL EDGE

R2

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB double-stack (Stafford+Adams+Egbuka from the game, Godwin R7 as a bring-back option) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final. Saquon adds PHI@SF if a SF bring-back is taken. Second QB from the R14-18 pool.
Hero-RB (Saquon) with LAR/TB mirror — opposite archetype to 6B (WR-forward DET) and different offenses from 6A (DAL/KC).
Slot 7Picks: 7, 18, 31…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI (newly ELITE) — Caleb R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5, CHI@DET env 81.0. NYG Dart R9 + Nabers R3 as the deep-value stack. TB pieces (Egbuka R3, Godwin R7) can be added QB-less as LAR@TB-game correlators.
THESIS
Slot 7 opens on the deep RB tier (James Cook / Ashton Jeanty / Chase Brown, adj11-14). We anchor an every-down back, pair a second RB (Derrick Henry on the quick wheel at pick 18), and stack KC (Mahomes+Rice) for a KC@LAC Week-17 game (env 76.6). Early-RB banks the BBM VI edge; WR ceiling fills behind.
Primary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy (R9-10)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R8, Worthy R10
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R8–R8 · prefer R8 (60% · +2.9, unavoidable-premium); Xavier Worthy R9–R9 · prefer R9 (67% · +5.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston (KC@LAC env 76.6)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R6, value)
Pieces: George Pickens (R3) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Pickens R3, Dak R6
Windows: Dak Prescott R6–R6 · prefer R6 (92% · +13.6, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice+Worthy, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Early-RB (Cook + Henry) with KC+DAL stacks. Distinct from 7B (WR-forward ARSB DET) and 7C (Hero-RB Jefferson leverage + LAR).
THESIS
Amon-Ra St. Brown (adj7) is reachable at pick 7 — anchor the premier Goff value stack (R9, score 0.894). ARSB+Jameson+Goff feeds a CHI@DET Week-17 game (env 81.0), plus a value Dallas secondary. WR-forward; RB1 by R7.

DET
QB: Jared Goff (R9, value adj103, qb_late)

W17 bring-back: CHI Colston Loveland (R3) / Rome Odunze (CHI@DET env 81.0)

W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Goff+ARSB+Jamo, bring back Loveland/Odunze, env 81.0) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces.
WR-forward DET home stack. Distinct from 7A (early-RB KC/DAL) and 7C (Hero-RB Jefferson leverage + LAR).
THESIS
Take Jonathan Taylor (adj8) as a single strong RB anchor at pick 7, then attack the LAR@TB mirror — Stafford (R7, value) + Adams from LAR, Egbuka (R3) + Godwin (R7) from TB, a premium Week-17 game env (77.8, second to CHI@DET). A Hero-RB build whose correlation lives entirely in one final-week game, distinct from 7A's KC/DAL and 7B's DET.
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (82% · +5.2, unavoidable-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R7 · prefer R7 (83% · +10.3, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Emeka Egbuka (R3) / Chris Godwin (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: (Mayfield unreachable at slot 7) Egbuka+Godwin as LAR-game correlators
Pieces: Emeka Egbuka (R3, value) + Chris Godwin (R7, value)
Rounds: Egbuka R3, Godwin R7
Windows: Emeka Egbuka R3–R3 · prefer R3 (66% · +1.6, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game — double stack)

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB single-game double-stack (Stafford+Adams+Egbuka; Godwin R7 as bring-back) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final. Second QB from the R12 pool. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Hero-RB (JT) with the LAR/TB single-game mirror. Distinct offenses from 7A (KC/DAL) and 7B (DET); archetype is RB-anchor vs 7B's WR-forward.
Slot 8Picks: 8, 17, 32…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI (newly ELITE) — Caleb R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5. NYG Dart R9 + Nabers R3 as the value stack. TB QB unreachable; Egbuka R3/Godwin R7 work QB-less as game correlators.
THESIS
Slot 8's R1 is deep RB (Jeanty/Chase Brown/Hampton, adj12-15); we anchor an every-down back and pair a second RB on the quick wheel (De'Von Achane / Derrick Henry at pick 17). Stack KC (Mahomes+Rice) for a KC@LAC Week-17 game (env 76.6). Early-RB banks the BBM VI edge.

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice+Worthy, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Pickens, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Early-RB (Jeanty + Achane) with KC+DAL stacks. Distinct from 8B (WR-forward Lamb DAL) and 8C (elite-TE Bowers leverage + LAR).
THESIS
CeeDee Lamb (adj10, ELITE Dallas) at pick 8 anchors a Dallas home stack: Lamb + Dak (R7, value) + Ferguson, carrying a DAL@NYG Week-17 game (env 73.1) with a Nabers bring-back. Secondary KC (Mahomes+Rice). WR-forward; RB1 by R7.

W17 CORRELATIONDAL@NYG (Dak+Lamb+Ferguson, bring back Nabers, env 73.1) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
WR-forward DAL home stack. Distinct from 8A (early-RB) and 8C (elite-TE Bowers leverage + LAR).
THESIS
Pick 8 can pair an RB anchor (Chase Brown, value adj14) with Brock Bowers (adj20) on the quick wheel (pick 17) — a rare early elite-TE leverage. WINNING_STRUCTURE flags elite TE as normally -EV; taken here only as a talent/target-share outlier. Correlate on LAR@TB (Stafford+Adams, Egbuka bring-back, env 77.8). RB1 met at R1.

LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7, value adj86)

TB
QB: (Mayfield unreachable at slot 8) Egbuka+Godwin as LAR-game correlators

Rounds: Egbuka R3, Godwin R7

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB double-stack (Stafford+Adams from LAR, Egbuka+Godwin from TB) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final. Bowers is a leverage TE. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Only slot-8 build with an early elite TE (Bowers) as leverage; LAR/TB stacks. Distinct from 8A (early-RB KC/DAL) and 8B (WR-forward DAL home).
Slot 9Picks: 9, 16, 33…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI (newly ELITE) — Caleb R6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5, replacing the KC secondary keeps a premium W17 game (CHI@DET 81.0). NYG Dart R9 + Nabers R3 as the deep-value alternative.
THESIS
Slot 9 can take Chase Brown (value adj14) at R1 and Derrick Henry on the quick wheel (pick 16) for two early RBs. Stack Green Bay — Love (R9, value, score 0.750) + Watson (R5) + Kraft (R7) — a cheap late-QB triple, with a value CIN bring-back off the Chase Brown anchor. Early-RB banks the BBM VI edge.
Primary Stack
GB
QB: Jordan Love (R9, ADP~112, value, qb_late)
Pieces: Christian Watson (R5, value) + Tucker Kraft (R7, value)

W17 CORRELATIONGB@HOU (Love+Watson+Kraft, bring back Collins/Schultz, env 55.2) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Early-RB (Chase Brown + Henry) with GB+KC stacks. Distinct from 9B (WR-forward Lamb DAL/PHI) and 9C (Hero-RB Hampton + CIN).
THESIS
CeeDee Lamb (adj10) at pick 9 anchors a Dallas home stack (Lamb+Dak R7+Ferguson, DAL@NYG env 73.1). Add a value Philadelphia secondary (Hurts R6 + DeVonta + Goedert) for a PHI@SF game (env 66.8). WR-forward; RB1 by R7.

DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R7, value)
Pieces: CeeDee Lamb (R1) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Lamb R1, Dak R7, Ferguson R11

W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)
Secondary Stack
PHI
QB: Jalen Hurts (R6, value)
Pieces: DeVonta Smith (R3, value) + Dallas Goedert (R10)
Rounds: DeVonta R3, Hurts R6
Windows: Jalen Hurts R6–R6 · prefer R6 (90% · +11.9, conscious-stack-premium); Dallas Goedert R10–R10 · prefer R10 (80% · +12.3, conscious-stack-premium)
W17 bring-back: SF George Kittle (PHI@SF env 66.8)

W17 CORRELATIONDAL@NYG (Dak+Lamb+Ferguson, bring back Nabers, env 73.1) and PHI@SF (Hurts+DeVonta+Goedert, bring back Kittle, env 66.8) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces.
WR-forward DAL+PHI stacks. Distinct from 9A (early-RB GB) and 9C (Hero-RB + CIN).
THESIS
Omarion Hampton (adj15, HIGH LAC) at pick 9 is a bell-cow rookie anchor; we bank him then attack the field-favored BAL@CIN game (Burrow+Higgins, bring back Andrews, env 75.9) with a SF secondary. Hero-RB satisfies the early-RB lean.

CIN
QB: Joe Burrow (R5, value window)
Pieces: Tee Higgins (R3, value)
Rounds: Higgins R3, Burrow R5
Windows: Tee Higgins R3–R3 · prefer R3 (72% · +2.4, unavoidable-premium); Joe Burrow R5–R5 · prefer R5 (61% · +2.1, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: BAL Mark Andrews (R11, value) — BAL@CIN env 75.9
Secondary Stack
SF
QB: Brock Purdy (R8, value)
Pieces: George Kittle (R9-10) / Ricky Pearsall
Rounds: Purdy R8, Kittle R9-10
Windows: Brock Purdy R8–R8 · prefer R8 (79% · +9.3, unavoidable-premium); George Kittle R9–R9 · prefer R9 (49% · -0.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: PHI DeVonta Smith (PHI@SF env 66.8)

RB1 R1QB1 R5TE1 R9
Joe Burrow taken R5 (pick 57) — CIN game-stack head for the BAL@CIN concentrator. R5 is his only window at slot 9. Conscious exception to QB-late.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN (Burrow+Higgins, bring back Andrews, env 75.9) and PHI@SF (Purdy+Kittle, bring back DeVonta, env 66.8) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Hero-RB (Hampton) with CIN+SF stacks. Distinct from 9A (early-RB GB) and 9B (WR-forward DAL/PHI).
Slot 10Picks: 10, 15, 34…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI is this slot's cheapest premium stack (newly ELITE; Caleb R5 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5) and 10A already runs it — treat 10A as the leverage build. Dart and Mayfield are unreachable here; TB Godwin R7 + McMillan R11 can be added QB-less as LAR@TB correlators.
THESIS
CeeDee Lamb (adj10, ELITE Dallas) at pick 10 anchors a WR-forward build. We stack Chicago (Caleb Williams R5 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5) — a value late-QB stack — that plays the highest-env Week-17 game (CHI@DET, env 81.0) and bring back Detroit's ARSB/Jamo. Dallas home pieces (Dak+Ferguson) form the secondary. RB1 by R7.
Primary Stack
CHI
QB: Caleb Williams (R5-6, ADP~70, value adj69)
Pieces: Colston Loveland (R3, value) + Rome Odunze (R5, value)
Rounds: Loveland R3, Odunze R5, Caleb R5-6
Windows: Colston Loveland R3–R4 · prefer R4 (56% · +0.8, flexible); Rome Odunze R5–R5 · prefer R5 (53% · +0.6, unavoidable-premium); Caleb Williams R5–R6 · prefer R6 (81% · +7.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: DET Amon-Ra St. Brown / Jameson Williams (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
DAL
QB: Dak Prescott (R7, value)
Pieces: CeeDee Lamb (R1) + Jake Ferguson (R11)
Rounds: Lamb R1, Dak R7
W17 bring-back: NYG Malik Nabers (env 73.1)

W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Caleb+Loveland+Odunze, bring back ARSB/Jamo, env 81.0 — highest) and DAL@NYG (Dak+Lamb+Ferguson, bring back Nabers) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces. Loveland R3 is an early TE taken as the game-stack bring-back at value.
WR-forward CHI+DAL stacks. Distinct from 10B (early-RB Saquon/K.Walker + LAR) and 10C (Hero-RB + CIN).
THESIS
The pick-10 turn (10/15) grabs two RBs fast: Saquon Barkley (adj13) then Kenneth Walker / De'Von Achane on the wheel. Two early RBs bank the strongest BBM VI signal (~30% advance). Correlate on the LAR@TB mirror (Stafford R7 + Adams, Egbuka+Godwin bring-back, env 77.8).
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7-8, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7-8
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (93% · +8.2, unavoidable-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R8 · prefer R7 (75% · +7.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Emeka Egbuka / Chris Godwin (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value) as LAR-game correlator
Pieces: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value)
Rounds: Godwin R7-8
Windows: Chris Godwin Jr. R7–R8 · prefer R8 (41% · -2.2, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game)

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB double-stack (Stafford+Adams from LAR, Godwin+Egbuka from TB) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final. Second QB from R14-18 pool. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Early-RB (Saquon + Walker) with LAR/TB mirror. Distinct from 10A (WR-forward CHI/DAL) and 10C (Hero-RB CIN).
THESIS
Chase Brown (value adj14, ELITE CIN) at pick 10 is both a bell-cow value anchor AND part of the Bengals offense — so his CIN stack (Burrow+Higgins) doubles as own-team correlation, in a BAL@CIN Week-17 game (env 75.9) with a Baltimore bring-back. SF (Purdy) secondary. Hero-RB satisfies the early-RB lean.
Primary Stack
CIN
QB: Joe Burrow (R5, value window)
Pieces: Tee Higgins (R3, value) + Chase Brown (RB anchor, R1 — own-team bonus)
Rounds: Higgins R3, Burrow R5
Windows: Tee Higgins R3–R3 · prefer R3 (63% · +1.4, unavoidable-premium); Joe Burrow R5–R5 · prefer R5 (56% · +1.1, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: BAL Mark Andrews (R11, value) — BAL@CIN env 75.9
Secondary Stack
SF
QB: Brock Purdy (R8, value)
Pieces: George Kittle (R9-10) / Ricky Pearsall
Rounds: Purdy R8, Kittle R9-10
Windows: Brock Purdy R8–R8 · prefer R8 (81% · +10.3, conscious-stack-premium); George Kittle R9–R9 · prefer R9 (46% · -1.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: PHI DeVonta Smith (PHI@SF env 66.8)

RB1 R1QB1 R5TE1 R9
Joe Burrow taken R5 — CIN stack head, own-team with the Chase Brown R1 anchor. R5 is his only window at slot 10. Conscious exception to QB-late.
W17 CORRELATIONBAL@CIN (Burrow+Higgins+Chase Brown, bring back Andrews, env 75.9) and PHI@SF (Purdy+Kittle, bring back DeVonta, env 66.8) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Hero-RB (Chase Brown) with CIN own-team + SF stacks. Distinct from 10A (WR-forward CHI) and 10B (early-RB LAR/TB).
Slot 11Picks: 11, 14, 35…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI (newly ELITE; Caleb R5 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5) — 11B already runs it; treat 11B as the leverage build. Dart/Mayfield unreachable from slot 11; TB Godwin R7 + McMillan R11 add QB-less LAR@TB correlation.
THESIS
The pick-11 turn (11/14) grabs two RBs fast: James Cook / Ashton Jeanty then Chase Brown / De'Von Achane on the wheel. Two early RBs bank the BBM VI edge. Stack Washington (Jayden Daniels R5-6, value + McLaurin R4) — a rushing-QB late stack — with a Dallas secondary. RB1 met at R1.
Primary Stack
WAS
QB: Jayden Daniels (R5-6, ADP~69, value adj68)
Pieces: Terry McLaurin (R4, value) + Chig Okonkwo (R12)
Rounds: McLaurin R4, Daniels R5-6
Windows: Terry McLaurin R4–R4 · prefer R4 (91% · +7.2, unavoidable-premium); Jayden Daniels R5–R6 · prefer R6 (81% · +7.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: JAX Brian Thomas Jr. / Travis Hunter (WAS@JAX env 37.6, low)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R7-8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R7-8
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R7–R8 · prefer R8 (73% · +6.9, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston (KC@LAC env 76.6)

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) is the stronger Week-17 game; WAS@JAX low-env so Daniels+McLaurin value is regular-season correlation. ~4-6 game-stacked pieces.
Early-RB (Jeanty + Chase Brown) with WAS+KC stacks. Distinct from 11B (WR-forward London/CHI) and 11C (Hero-RB Saquon + LAR).
THESIS
Drake London (adj16, value) at pick 11 is a target-hog WR anchor. Stack Chicago (Caleb R5-6 + Loveland R3 + Odunze R5) for the highest-env Week-17 game (CHI@DET, env 81.0) with a Detroit bring-back. KC secondary. RB1 by R7.
Primary Stack
CHI
QB: Caleb Williams (R5-6, value adj69)
Pieces: Colston Loveland (R3, value) + Rome Odunze (R5, value)
Rounds: Loveland R3, Odunze R5, Caleb R5-6
Windows: Colston Loveland R3–R4 · prefer R4 (64% · +1.8, flexible); Rome Odunze R5–R5 · prefer R5 (48% · -0.4, flexible); Caleb Williams R5–R6 · prefer R6 (84% · +8.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: DET Amon-Ra St. Brown / Jameson Williams (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R7-8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R7-8
Windows: Rashee Rice R2–R2 · prefer R2 (100% · +11.1, conscious-stack-premium); Patrick Mahomes R7–R8 · prefer R8 (73% · +6.9, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Johnston (env 76.6)

W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Caleb+Loveland+Odunze, bring back ARSB/Jamo, env 81.0 — highest) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces. Loveland R3 is an early TE taken as the game-stack bring-back at value.
WR-forward CHI+KC stacks. Distinct from 11A (early-RB WAS) and 11C (Hero-RB LAR).
THESIS
Omarion Hampton (adj15, HIGH LAC) at pick 11 is a bell-cow rookie anchor; we bank him then attack the LAR@TB mirror (Stafford R7 + Adams, Egbuka+Godwin bring-back, env 77.8). Hero-RB satisfies the early-RB lean; correlation lives in a premium Week-17 game env.
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7-8, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7-8
Windows: Davante Adams R4–R4 · prefer R4 (95% · +9.2, unavoidable-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R8 · prefer R7 (72% · +6.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: TB Chris Godwin (R7-8) / Emeka Egbuka (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value) as LAR-game correlator
Pieces: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value)
Rounds: Godwin R7-8
Windows: Chris Godwin Jr. R7–R8 · prefer R8 (45% · -1.2, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game)

RB1 R1QB1 R7TE1 R11
W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB double-stack (Stafford+Adams from LAR, Godwin from TB) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final; SF (Purdy) 2nd QB adds the PHI@SF game. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Hero-RB (Hampton) with LAR/TB mirror + SF. Distinct from 11A (early-RB WAS/KC) and 11B (WR-forward CHI).
Slot 12Picks: 12, 13, 36…
LEVERAGE PIVOTDe-chalk pivot: CHI (newly ELITE; Caleb R5 + Loveland R3-4 + Odunze R5) — 12B already runs it; treat 12B as the leverage build. Dart/Mayfield unreachable; TB Godwin R7 + McMillan R11 add QB-less LAR@TB correlation.
THESIS
Slot 12 picks 12 AND 13 back-to-back — the classic turn double. We take two RB anchors immediately (Saquon/Hampton/K.Walker), the strongest early-RB signal (two consecutive RBs ~30% advance in BBM VI). Then a value KC stack (Mahomes R7 + Rice R2) for a KC@LAC Week-17 game (env 76.6), with an SF secondary.
Primary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R7-8, ADP~93, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R2, value) + Xavier Worthy (R9-10)
Rounds: Rice R2, Mahomes R7-8, Worthy R10
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R7–R8 · prefer R8 (76% · +7.9, flexible); Xavier Worthy R9–R9 · prefer R9 (52% · +0.7, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Quentin Johnston (KC@LAC env 76.6)
Secondary Stack
SF
QB: Brock Purdy (R7-8, value)
Pieces: George Kittle (R9-10) / Ricky Pearsall
Rounds: Purdy R7-8, Kittle R9-10
Windows: Brock Purdy R7–R7 · prefer R7 (87% · +13.3, flexible); George Kittle R9–R10 · prefer R10 (37% · -4.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: PHI DeVonta Smith (PHI@SF env 66.8)

W17 CORRELATIONKC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice+Worthy, bring back Johnston, env 76.6) and PHI@SF (Purdy+Kittle, bring back DeVonta, env 66.8) give two Week-17 games; ~5-7 correlated pieces.
Early-RB turn double (Saquon + Hampton) with KC+SF stacks. Distinct from 12B (WR-forward London/Bowers + CHI) and 12C (Hero-RB Jeanty + LAR).
THESIS
The 12/13 turn takes an RB anchor (Kenneth Walker, ELITE KC) then Brock Bowers (adj20, elite TE) back-to-back — the turn is the one place to grab an every-down back and a top-2 TE in consecutive picks. Bowers is a conscious leverage TE (WINNING_STRUCTURE flags elite TE -EV) taken as a talent outlier. Stack Chicago (Caleb R5-6 + Loveland R4 + Odunze R5) for the CHI@DET game (env 81.0). RB1 met at R1.
Primary Stack
CHI
QB: Caleb Williams (R5-6, value adj69)
Pieces: Colston Loveland (R4, value) + Rome Odunze (R5, value)
Rounds: Loveland R4, Odunze R5, Caleb R5-6
Windows: Colston Loveland R3–R4 · prefer R4 (72% · +2.8, unavoidable-premium); Rome Odunze R5–R5 · prefer R5 (42% · -1.4, flexible); Caleb Williams R5–R6 · prefer R6 (86% · +9.3, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: DET Amon-Ra St. Brown / Jameson Williams (CHI@DET env 81.0)
Secondary Stack
KC
QB: Patrick Mahomes (R7-8, value)
Pieces: Rashee Rice (R3, value)
Rounds: Rice R3, Mahomes R7-8
Windows: Patrick Mahomes R7–R8 · prefer R8 (76% · +7.9, unavoidable-premium)
W17 bring-back: LAC Johnston (env 76.6)

RB1 R1QB1 R6TE1 R2
W17 CORRELATIONCHI@DET (Caleb+Loveland+Odunze, bring back ARSB/Jamo, env 81.0 — highest) and KC@LAC (Mahomes+Rice, bring back Johnston) give two Week-17 games; ~6-8 correlated pieces. Bowers + Loveland = 2 TE early, a deliberate leverage/bring-back double.
Elite-TE leverage turn (Walker + Bowers) + CHI/KC stacks. Distinct from 12A (early-RB double KC/SF) and 12C (Hero-RB LAR).
THESIS
Ashton Jeanty (adj12) at pick 12 is an every-down anchor; we take one strong RB then pivot the turn's second pick to WR and attack the LAR@TB mirror (Stafford R7 + Adams, Godwin+Egbuka bring-back, env 77.8). A Hero-RB build whose correlation lives entirely in the top Week-17 game.
Primary Stack
LAR
QB: Matthew Stafford (R7-8, value adj86)
Pieces: Davante Adams (R4, value)
Rounds: Adams R4, Stafford R7-8
Windows: Davante Adams R3–R4 · prefer R4 (96% · +10.2, conscious-stack-premium); Matthew Stafford R7–R8 · prefer R8 (66% · +4.3, flexible)
W17 bring-back: TB Chris Godwin (R7-8) / Emeka Egbuka (LAR@TB env 77.8)
Secondary Stack
TB
QB: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value) as LAR-game correlator
Pieces: Chris Godwin Jr. (R7-8, value)
Rounds: Godwin R7-8
Windows: Chris Godwin Jr. R7–R7 · prefer R7 (53% · +0.8, flexible)
W17 bring-back: LAR Adams (same game)

W17 CORRELATIONLAR@TB double-stack (Stafford+Adams from LAR, Godwin from TB) — 4-5 players in a 77.8-env final; 2nd QB (Jordan Love R9) adds the GB@HOU game. ~5-7 game-stacked pieces.
Hero-RB (Jeanty) with LAR/TB mirror. Distinct from 12A (early-RB turn double KC/SF) and 12B (WR/TE-forward CHI).
